evals.description: Trigger test: activates on faceted navigation crawl question
evals.prompt: Our Shopify store has 8,000 products and we use filters for color, size, brand, and price. Google is showing 400,000 pages indexed in Search Console but we only have 8,000 products. What's happening and how do we fix it?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on product schema / rich snippet question
evals.prompt: Our competitors show star ratings and price in Google search results but our products don't. How do we get those rich snippets for our product pages?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on out-of-stock SEO question
evals.prompt: We have about 200 products that go out of stock periodically. Our developer wants to delete those pages and 404 them to keep the site clean. Is that the right call from an SEO perspective?

evals.description: Core task (code): generate valid Product schema with Offer and AggregateRating
evals.prompt: Write the JSON-LD Product schema markup for a product page selling a Sony WH-1000XM5 headphone priced at $349.99, currently in stock, with 4.8 average rating from 1,847 reviews. The store is called AudioZone and the product URL is https://audiozone.com/products/sony-wh1000xm5.

evals.description: Core task (code): implement noindex on filtered pages only
evals.prompt: I need to add a noindex meta tag to filtered category pages (URLs with ?color=, ?size=, ?sort= parameters) but NOT on base category pages or product pages. Show me how to do this in a server-side rendered Node.js/Express app.

evals.description: Core task (explanation): canonical strategy for paginated category pages
evals.prompt: For our paginated category pages like /womens/boots?page=2 and /womens/boots?page=3, should we set the canonical to /womens/boots (page 1) or should each page canonical to itself? I've seen conflicting advice online.

evals.description: Core task (code): breadcrumb schema for a product page
evals.prompt: Generate the BreadcrumbList schema markup for a product page at: Home > Women's > Shoes > Boots > UGG Classic Short Boot. Include the HTML breadcrumb nav with microdata and a separate JSON-LD block.

evals.description: Core task (explanation): product variant consolidation vs separate URLs
evals.prompt: We sell a t-shirt that comes in 12 colors and 6 sizes. Right now each color has its own URL like /products/basic-tee-black and /products/basic-tee-red. Is it better to keep separate URLs per color, or consolidate them all under /products/basic-tee with a color picker?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns that rel=next/prev is deprecated
evals.prompt: I'm implementing pagination on our category pages and found old SEO guides recommending rel='next' and rel='prev' tags. Should I add those to our paginated category pages?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against lazy-loading all product images without fallback
evals.prompt: For performance, our developer wants to JavaScript lazy-load all product images on category pages so they only load when in the viewport. Is there an SEO risk to this?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns that duplicate manufacturer descriptions cause thin content issues
evals.prompt: We're launching a new electronics store and our supplier is giving us full product descriptions for all 3,000 products. Can we just use the supplier's copy directly to save time on content creation?

assertions.value: yes, you can use the supplier's copy

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not recommend blocking Googlebot from all category pages
evals.prompt: Someone told us we should block all category listing pages in robots.txt because they're 'not real content' and only product pages matter for SEO. Is that right?

evals.description: References load: points to faceted-navigation reference file for robots.txt blocking strategy
evals.prompt: I need to block our ?sort= and ?page_size= URL parameters from being crawled by Googlebot. Can you walk me through the robots.txt rules and also show me how to handle the case where parameters appear in different orders in the URL?
